title: PUT /api/settings accepts absent benches.maxGlobal (unlimited)
preconditions: Server is running with no existing settings.json

steps.action: PUT /api/settings with body { theme: 'dark' } (no benches block)
expected: Response body has no benches.maxGlobal field

title: PUT /api/settings persists benches.maxGlobal = 5

steps.action: PUT /api/settings with body { theme: 'dark', benches: { maxGlobal: 5 } }

title: PUT /api/settings rejects benches.maxGlobal = 0 with 400

expected: Error message names the offending field benches.maxGlobal

title: PUT /api/settings rejects benches.maxGlobal = -3 with 400
PUT /api/settings rejects benches.maxGlobal = 2.5 with 400
createBench succeeds when global count < cap

expected: Bench returned with status preparing or initialised

title: createBench returns 409 GLOBAL_CAP_REACHED when global count = cap

expected: Message names current count (3) and cap (3)
Map size is still 3 (no leaked entry)

title: In-flight bench reservation is visible to a parallel create attempt

steps.action: Begin createBench A; pause inside async provisioning (status = preparing)
expected: Map size is now 4 (slot reserved before await)

steps.action: While A is still in flight, call createBench B

title: clearBench is allowed even when global count equals cap
Settings UI renders the tab as 'Benches' (renamed)

expected: No tab labelled 'Bench Defaults' is visible at the top level

title: BenchesTab renders Global bench limit RadioGroup and numeric TextField

expected: A RadioGroup labelled 'Limit mode' (or equivalent) shows Unlimited (selected) and Limit options
Numeric TextField is disabled while Unlimited is selected
Default value populates (e.g. 5) or empty awaiting input

title: 'Set up bench' button has aria-disabled=true with descriptive tooltip when at cap

expected: Button has aria-disabled='true' (not the native disabled attribute)
Tooltip text reads: 'Global bench limit reached. 2 of 2 benches in use. Clear a bench to free a slot.'

title: GlobalBenchMeter renders only when a cap is set

expected: No GlobalBenchMeter element is in the DOM

steps.action: Set benches.maxGlobal = 5 via Settings and return to BenchesTab
expected: GlobalBenchMeter is rendered in the header strip

title: GlobalBenchMeter exposes aria-label 'Global benches: N of M'
Lowering cap below current count is accepted and preserves existing benches
createBench succeeds and warns once when settings.json is corrupt (fail-open)

expected: console.warn called exactly once with message including '[bench-manager]' and 'settings.json'

steps.action: Call createBench again in the same process
expected: console.warn count is still 1 (one warning per process load)

title: Error-state benches count toward the cap
Existing install with no maxGlobal behaves unchanged (backcompat)
preconditions: ~/.roubo/settings.json has no benches block at all

title: Cap-check on createBench adds no I/O beyond a single loadSettings

expected: loadSettings called exactly once during the cap-check path
No additional fs reads attributable to the cap check

title: PUT /api/settings remains unauthenticated (no new auth surface)

expected: Request is accepted (single-user desktop posture preserved)

title: End-to-end: user sets a cap, creates benches up to it, button disables, clears one, re-enables
preconditions: Fresh install, no cap, no benches

steps.action: Open Settings > Benches; select Limit; enter 2; Save
Navigate to BenchesTab; create bench A

expected: 'Set up bench' button aria-disabled with cap-reached tooltip
GlobalBenchMeter shows '1 of 2' in neutral colour

title: End-to-end: user lowers cap below current count, meter goes red, clearing restores capacity

steps.action: Open Settings > Benches; switch to Limit; enter 3; Save
expected: Save succeeds; meter shows '5 of 3' in red

steps.action: Attempt to create a new bench
expected: Server returns 409 GLOBAL_CAP_REACHED; UI surfaces a clear error
After the last clear the meter shows '2 of 3' in neutral and 'Set up bench' re-enables

title: Two concurrent create requests at boundary: exactly one succeeds

steps.action: Fire POST /api/projects/proj-a/benches and POST /api/projects/proj-b/benches in parallel
expected: Exactly one returns 201; the other returns 409 GLOBAL_CAP_REACHED

title: Upgrade path: existing user with no settings.benches sees unchanged behaviour, then opts in
preconditions: Fresh server pointed at a settings.json from a prior version (no benches block); 3 existing benches

steps.action: Open Settings > Benches; set cap to 4; Save
expected: Meter appears showing '4 of 4'; 'Set up bench' button disables

title: Per-project cap takes precedence when tighter than global
preconditions: Project proj-a has benches.max = 2 in roubo.yaml
proj-a already has 2 benches; other projects empty

expected: Per-project rejection surfaces with the existing per-project error (not GLOBAL_CAP_REACHED)

title: PUT /api/settings rejects benches.maxGlobal = Infinity

steps.action: PUT /api/settings with body { benches: { maxGlobal: Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY } } (or sent as a stringified value that coerces to Infinity)

title: PUT /api/settings rejects benches.maxGlobal = NaN

steps.action: PUT /api/settings with a body where benches.maxGlobal coerces to NaN (e.g. a value of 'abc' after Number())

title: PUT /api/settings rejects benches.maxGlobal as a string
Lowering cap exactly to current count blocks new creates
First launch with no settings.json treats cap as unlimited

steps.action: Start the server and call createBench
expected: No warning logged (file absence is not corruption)

title: Switching the RadioGroup from Limit back to Unlimited clears the numeric input and removes the cap

expected: Numeric TextField becomes disabled and visually cleared
PUT /api/settings payload includes benches.maxGlobal = null (or absent)

title: Same-tick concurrent createBench calls at cap-1 do not exceed the cap

steps.action: Invoke createBench twice synchronously (Promise.all) targeting different projects
expected: Exactly one fulfils with 201; the other rejects with GLOBAL_CAP_REACHED

title: aria-disabled 'Set up bench' button is focusable and its tooltip is announced

steps.action: Tab focus onto the 'Set up bench' button
expected: Focus ring visible per project tokens

steps.action: Inspect computed accessible name and description
expected: Accessible description includes the cap-reached tooltip text

title: GlobalBenchMeter colour ramp transitions at the documented thresholds

steps.action: Set bench count = 7 (70%); inspect meter fill
expected: Fill uses neutral colour token (stone-400 or equivalent)

steps.action: Set bench count = 8 (80%); inspect meter fill
Set bench count = 10 (100%); inspect meter fill
Set bench count = 12 (over cap after lowering); inspect meter fill
expected: Fill remains red-500 and capped at 100% width; count text shows 12 of 10

title: Stale client click hits server cap and CreateBenchModal surfaces the 409 message
preconditions: Client cache stale; UI still shows 'Set up bench' enabled

steps.action: User clicks 'Set up bench' and submits the modal
expected: Modal renders the server error via existing setError flow; modal does not crash

title: Property test: random caps and random create patterns never exceed the cap

steps.action: For 1000 random samples of (cap in [1,1000], creates in [0, 2*cap], clears interleaved): drive createBench/clearBench
expected: Map size never exceeds cap at any observation point
No createBench call returns 201 when size >= cap at reservation time

title: 100 sequential create+clear cycles at boundary do not leak Map entries

steps.action: Loop 100 times: clear one bench, then create one bench

title: Exploratory: prototype scenarios render identically when wired to real state
preconditions: Prototype scenarios from .specifications/global-bench-limit/prototype/index.html known: unlimited, below, near, at, lowered

steps.action: For each scenario, configure real settings + bench state to match and visually compare the production UI to the prototype
expected: Production UI matches the prototype's meter, tooltip text, button state, and project tile counts within reasonable tolerance
